label: Distributional welfare weighting parameters (OMB A-4 revised)

elasticity_marginal_utility.label: Elasticity of marginal utility of income (e)
elasticity_marginal_utility.note: The revised A-4 (2023) formally endorses income-based distributional weights. Elasticity of 1.4 is slightly higher than the UK Green Book (1.3), reflecting the wider US income distribution.

status: The revised A-4 makes distributional analysis a formal expectation for significant regulatory actions (>$100M annual impact). This is a major change from the pre-2023 A-4 which treated distributional analysis as optional.

source.publication: OMB Circular A-4: Regulatory Analysis (Revised November 2023), Section IV.D
source.update_frequency: A-4 revisions are infrequent (last major revision before 2023 was 2003)

caveats: Distributional analysis is supplementary. The unweighted NPV remains the primary metric.
The A-4 revision's distributional requirements may be subject to executive order changes.
Income data from ACS is lagged by 1-2 years. Use the most recent available.
The 1.4 elasticity is higher than most international estimates (UK 1.3, EU 1.0-1.2). This means US distributional weights give proportionally more weight to low-income groups.
